programmes.show_description: Cosmopolitan Comfort. This trip, Guy Fieri tucks into cosmopolitan comfort food. He samples corned beef and cabbage, Ethiopian specialties, and lamb poutine-loaded hot dogs! S25, Ep7
Burgers, Buns And Bagels. Guy Fieri is going carb-crazy this trip! He finds a deli serving up lamb burgers and ribs. And, a Koreatown eatery makes Asian buns and a bacon-corn dish. S25, Ep8
Big Food, Small Towns. Guy Fieri visits small towns with big food! He checks out a butchery cranking out corn dogs and a 1700s inn-turned-gastropub serving a spin on disco fries. S25, Ep9
From Port To Port. This trip, Guy Fieri goes from Portsmouth to Portland looking for top flavours! He checks out octopus, classic biscuits and gravy, and pork belly tacos. S25, Ep10
Des Moines. Adam Richman is in Des Moines, Iowa, for some Midwestern comfort food, unique sandwich-inspired pizza and the five-pound Adam Emmenecker sandwich challenge. S2, Ep38
Knoxville. Adam Richman is in Knoxville, Tennessee, trying out some monumental hand-cut steaks, unique burgers and a five-pound barbecue burrito challenge. S2, Ep39
Sandals, Sandwiches & Shelves. A client orders a giant flip flop cake and the team happily obliges. Later a school asks Buddy to bake a scrummy kid-friendly cake for their students. S3, Ep17
Pipes, A Phoenix & A Problematic Kitchen. Buddy bakes a Phoenix Cake, but it's hard work since the ovens are being replaced. Joey tests out the new ovens by making Irish Soda Bread. S3, Ep18
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Not Your Typical Joints. Guy checks out restaurants with funky dishes and unique specialties. They include an eatery tucked inside a beer store serving crazy waffle creations! S26, Ep7
Multicultural Cooking. Guy Fieri digs into the gastronomic traditions of different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida. S26, Ep11
From Spicy To Icy. Guy Fieri heats up with spice and cools off with ice! He visits a hot BBQ joint in California, a Cajun spot in Virginia and a Hawaiian place's tropical shaved ice. S26, Ep15
Creole, Cold Cuts And Crepes. Guy Fieri samples gumbo in LA, a Minneapolis deli serving meatless meats and a Hawaiian noodle spot dishing out ramen and Vietnamese crepes. S25, Ep3
Sweet Charity. Lunch with Ina is up for grabs for lucky auction winners. The successful bidders can look forward to a delicious lunch of chilled cucumber soup and seafood salad. S4, Ep3
Jeffrey's Dinner. Ina is cooking a delicious steak dinner for Jeffrey, but he's in charge of the grilling. She also makes a herby ricotta bruschetta and tasty watermelon mojitos. S4, Ep4
Old School Retro Mix. Ina takes a trip down memory lane and updates some classic recipes. She cooks a meatloaf with chef Kevin Penner and modernises a smoked salmon devilled eggs. S4, Ep5
Easy French. Ina visits the legendary chef, Daniel Boulud, at his flagship restaurant in New York. Together they create one of his delicious, signature, classic, French dishes. S4, Ep6
Cook Like A Pro. Ina shares some of her top-tips that she learnt as a professional chef. She makes a classic lemon chicken breast dish and a mouth-watering sticky toffee date cake. S4, Ep7
Easy Desserts. Ina makes easy sweet treats like her delicious fruit-filled cranberry and apple cake. Plus, she gets a dessert masterclass from pastry chef, Claudia Fleming. S4, Ep8
Bakes To Share. Nadiya Hussain makes baking treats to share with loved ones. She starts with a chilli and cranberry brioche wreath that features an entire baked camembert cheese. S1, Ep5
Baking On A Budget. Nadiya Hussain shares some cheap recipes that are rich in flavour. They include a creamy potato rosti quiche, onion pretzels and Nadiya's chocolate fondant. S1, Ep6
Biscuits And Bites. Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite biscuits and bites. She starts with her own spin on the classic amaretti biscuit before making savoury chicken doughnuts. S1, Ep7
Celebration Bakes. Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite party cakes for any occasion, including an eight-layered honey cake and her colourful Mardi Gras-inspired king cake. S1, Ep8
James's adventure takes him to the Camargue. He learns of the fleur de sel, a delicate salty crust which is harvested in the waters of Aigues Mortes. S1, Ep13
James journeys to Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and home to Vincent Van Gogh. He cooks a delicious dish using food from the local market stalls. S1, Ep14
The Bikers cook the perfect dishes for cosy suppers. They give a new take on classic steak and chips, serve up delicious Indonesian pork stew, and a sticky gingerbread. S1, Ep1
Si and Dave cook dishes that turn up the heat! They give a spicy twist on a family favourite with roasted spiced cauliflower cheese, and Caribbean-inspired jerk pork chops. S1, Ep2
Dave enjoys a taste of his native county, Cumbria, as he and Si cook recipes with local ingredients. Dishes include a deliciously decadent sticky toffee pudding. S1, Ep3
The Bikers cook the perfect dishes to share with the people we love. On the menu are baked cheesecake, a crowd-pleasing terrine and the ultimate beef sandwich. S1, Ep4
Iceland. Adam seeks out off-menu eats in Reykjavik, Iceland. Dishes include a meatball sandwich at a laundromat, and traditional Icelandic delicacies hidden in a bus station. S2, Ep15
Greenland. Adam Richman looks for hidden eats in Greenland. Dishes include reindeer schnitzel, and fried rice that combines Thai and Greenlandic flavours. S2, Ep16
Bologna. Adam's in Bologna tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including Bolognese sauce and a sweet made with rice and almonds. S1, Ep7
Naples. Adam's in Naples tasting iconic foods named after this city, including the classic Napolitina pizza and a pasta dish called spaghetti alle vongole. S1, Ep8
Sauerkraut, Shoyu And Shank. Guy Fieri's grabbing flavour combos that pack a punch, like Greek grub in Naples, pork belly lollipops in Dallas, and next-level ramen in Chicago. S41, Ep3
African, Sicilian And Sausage. Guy Fieri's diving into Kenyan curried goat in Tallahassee, béchamel arancini in Chicago, and a legit lamb platter in Gainesville. S41, Ep4
Chicken Trifecta. Guy Fieri hits the road for top chicken dinners. In Hawaii visits Mick Fleetwood's restaurant and he checks out a California hot dog spot's chicken and waffles. S26, Ep14
Asia, Africa And India. Guy Fieri tucks into big international flavours. He visits a Filipino joint in LA, samples Ethiopian cuisine and enjoys Indian classics in Minneapolis. S25, Ep2
Turkey-Giving. Guy Fieri prepares for Turkey Day! In Princeton, NJ, rapper Rev. Run jumps in for crepes and a New Hampshire cafe cooks up terrific turkey meatloaf. S25, Ep11
Brunch, Bologna And Burgers. Host Guy tries out fried bologna and veggie burgers in Louisville, KY, and brunch staples at a diner-restaurant mashup in Portland, OR. S25, Ep12
Burgers, Buns And Bagels. Guy Fieri is going carb-crazy this trip! He finds a deli serving up lamb burgers and ribs. And, a Koreatown eatery makes Asian buns and a bacon-corn dish. S25, Ep8
Not Your Typical Joints. Guy checks out restaurants with funky dishes and unique specialties. They include an eatery tucked inside a beer store serving crazy waffle creations! S26, Ep7
Kickin' It In Cancun. Guy Fieri feels the heat in Mexico! He checks out a family-owned spot where grandma cooks up rellenos. And, Guy shows off his Playa Del Carmen restaurant. S26, Ep6
Wonder Women. Guy Fieri visits all-female line-ups in Houston and Orlando. A grandma serves up family recipes like homemade tamales, and he checks out a funky meatball spot. S26, Ep10
Multicultural Cooking. Guy Fieri digs into the gastronomic traditions of different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida. S26, Ep11
From Spicy To Icy. Guy Fieri heats up with spice and cools off with ice! He visits a hot BBQ joint in California, a Cajun spot in Virginia and a Hawaiian place's tropical shaved ice. S26, Ep15

From North To South America. From a Houston joint's Argentinian empanadas to street tacos in Cancun, Mexico, Guy Fieri digs into flavours spanning the Americas. S26, Ep8
Turkey, Taters And Dogs. Guy digs into a mashup of flavours, including a potato-centric palace dishing out hearty Brazilian specialties and a deli serving latkes and turkey pastrami. S26, Ep12
Island And Desert. Guy Fieri hunts desert and island flavour. He enjoys nostalgic breakfast tarts at a café in California and fish and chips at a gluten-free spot in Hawaii. S26, Ep16
Chew And Brew. Guy Fieri samples pub food in Minneapolis, Miami and LA, from wild boar gnocchi to a lamb belly BLT. And, he meets Harry Wayne Casey from KC and The Sunshine Band! S25, Ep4
Kansas City. Adam Richman is in Kansas City for some world-class barbecue, some of the best pan-fried chicken in the USA and the six-pound Ultimate Destroyer sandwich! S2, Ep30
Jersey Shore. Adam is at the Jersey Shore checking out a Trenton-style tomato pie and mighty octopus-shaped ice cream sundae. Then, he tries an insanely spicy wing-eating challenge. S2, Ep32
Portland, ME. Adam Richman visits the land of lighthouses and lobsters: Portland, Maine. He samples lobster right out of the pot and some of the best coastal views imaginable. S2, Ep34
Butte. Adam tries a local staple in Butte, Montana: the pasty, a delicious meat-and-vegetable-filled pastry whose culinary tradition is as old as the mines. S2, Ep36
Des Moines. Adam Richman is in Des Moines, Iowa, for some Midwestern comfort food, unique sandwich-inspired pizza and the five-pound Adam Emmenecker sandwich challenge. S2, Ep38
Indianapolis. Foodie Adam Richman is in Indianapolis, Indiana, for the Indy staple pork tenderloin sandwich, some Hoosier home cooking and the enormous Big Ugly Burger Challenge. S2, Ep31
Syracuse. Adam Richman is in Syracuse, NY, checking out some serious grub. He tries the pork-sket sandwich at a biker BBQ joint. Then he takes on a monumental frittata challenge. S2, Ep33
Niagara Falls. Adam gets romantic at Niagara Falls. Adam has a date with a steak that barely fits on a plate. Then he crosses the border for the Italian Challenge. S2, Ep35
Bologna. Adam's in Bologna tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including Bolognese sauce and a sweet made with rice and almonds. S1, Ep7
Naples. Adam's in Naples tasting iconic foods named after this city, including the classic Napolitina pizza and a pasta dish called spaghetti alle vongole. S1, Ep8
James heads to the riverside town of Isle Sur La Sorge, where his food hero, Keith Floyd, once owned a restaurant. He cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. S1, Ep15
James travels to Normandy, home to his all-time favourite ingredient, butter! He cooks up artichokes with hollandaise whilst visiting a Calvados orchard. S1, Ep16
It's Bread Week and the bakers prove themselves in a sweet and savoury signature. There's a special technical that honours the NHS and an artistic showstopper. S11, Ep3
In Chocolate Week, and the bakers face a deceptively simple traybake signature. There's a chocolate and nut-filled technical and a white chocolate showstopper. S11, Ep4
It's Pastry Week and the bakers put their twist on a Cornish classic. They go retro in the technical and make a tart hidden within a cage made of pastry! S11, Ep5
It's Japanese Week and the bakers tackle steamed buns and a layered technical challenge. Plus, a showstopper is inspired by kawaii - the Japanese love of all things cute! S11, Ep6
Dave Myers and Si King explore the food of the American South. They start in Memphis, Tennessee, where they try iconic Southern dishes: a soul stew and fried chicken. S1, Ep1
Blues. The bikers head to New Orleans, where they meet blues musician Little Freddie King and share their own take on a traditional, Southern classic - the po' boy sandwich. S1, Ep2
